skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5-120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that produces short videos through the Pexo platform.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 534 patterns across 13 files, yielding a risk score of 100/100. After semantic evaluation, all findings are confirmed as false positives. The skill is a legitimate API client for the Pexo video platform. Shell command substitution patterns are standard bash scripting for API interaction. Network URLs all point to pexo.ai (the documented service). Filesystem access targets ~/.pexo/config (the documented configuration path). Environment variable access reads user-provided PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL for authentication. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was detected. The only risk is plaintext storage of API credentials in a local config file, which is standard practice for this type of tool.

low_findings.title: Plaintext API key storage
low_findings.description: API credentials (PEXO_API_KEY) are stored in plaintext in ~/.pexo/config. This is standard for CLI tools but means anyone with filesystem access to the user's home directory could read the key.

low_findings.title: Temporary file creation
low_findings.description: Scripts create temporary files using mktemp for HTTP request/response handling. Temp files are cleaned up after each request, but a crash could leave sensitive data (API responses) on disk.

low_findings.title: Direct file path from user input
low_findings.description: The upload script accepts a file path directly from command arguments without path traversal validation. This is acceptable for a local CLI tool but should be documented.

content.user_title: Create AI-powered short videos with Pexo
content.value_statement: Producing professional-quality videos typically requires expensive tools and design expertise. This skill connects AI coding assistants to the Pexo video platform, enabling automated video creation from natural language descriptions.
actual_capabilities: Create 5 to 120 second videos in 16:9, 9:16, or 1:1 aspect ratios
Upload images, video clips, and audio files as reference material
Poll project status and handle interactive preview selections
Manage project lifecycle including creation, revisions, and delivery
Display credit balance and guide users through purchase flow
Download final video files and provide shareable URLs
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key and active credit balance on the Pexo platform
Video production takes 15 to 20 minutes for a short video and cannot be accelerated
Pexo cannot crawl external web URLs; files must be downloaded locally before uploading
Requires curl, jq, and file utilities installed on the system

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: A social media manager generates product ads, TikTok clips, and Instagram Reels by describing the desired content. The skill handles file uploads, project management, and video delivery automatically.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Marketing video production
use_cases.description: A marketing team creates brand videos and explainer videos without hiring a video production team. They describe the message, upload brand assets, and receive a finished video.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and small business owners

use_cases.title: Rapid video prototyping
use_cases.description: A product designer tests video concepts by quickly generating multiple variations. They describe different styles and review preview options to select the best version.

prompt_templates.title: Simple video request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 15-second video about a coffee shop opening in the morning. Warm, cozy atmosphere.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - User wants a simple video with a clear description

prompt_templates.title: Product video with uploaded image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a product ad video for these sneakers. Use the uploaded image as the hero shot. Modern, energetic style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - User provides a reference image for the video

prompt_templates.title: Video with revisions
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but make the background darker and add more contrast to the product.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - User wants to revise an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-language video request
prompt_templates.prompt: 做个猫的视频，要可爱的风格，10秒左右。
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - User writes in a non-English language; the skill relays the request exactly as-is to Pexo

output_examples.input: Make a product video for my new headphones
output: Project created successfully. Project ID: proj_abc123
Your request has been submitted to Pexo. Estimated time: 15-20 minutes. Project page: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123
Revision submitted for project proj_abc123. Pexo is updating your video. Estimated time: 10-15 minutes. Project page: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

best_practices: Describe the video you want in natural language. Pexo will ask for any missing details about duration, style, or aspect ratio.
Upload reference files (images, audio, video) before sending your message. Pexo cannot access external URLs.
Do not send additional messages to Pexo while the project status is WAIT. This triggers duplicate video production.
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details that the user did not specify. This overrides Pexo's professional judgment with guesses.
Do not call pexo-chat.sh while the project is in WAIT status. Only poll with pexo-project-get.sh during this phase.
Do not share truncated download URLs. Always send the full URL with all query parameters, or the link will return a 403 error.

faq.question: What does Pexo cost?
faq.answer: Pexo uses a credit-based pricing model. Each video creation consumes credits from your account. You can check your balance by running pexo-entitlements.sh or visiting pexo.ai/home.

faq.question: How long does video creation take?
faq.answer: A 15-second video typically takes 15 to 20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time. The skill polls for status every 60 seconds and updates you on progress.

faq.question: Can Pexo use files from a web URL?
faq.answer: No. Pexo cannot crawl external URLs. You must download the file to your local machine first, then use the upload function to include it in your video project.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, jpeg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic, heif. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: What happens when my credits run out?
faq.answer: When credits are insufficient, the skill automatically detects this and displays your balance with a purchase link. Visit pexo.ai/home, click Credits in the top-right corner, and select Buy Credits.

faq.question: How do I set up Pexo for the first time?
faq.answer: Create a config file at ~/.pexo/config with your PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. Then run pexo-doctor.sh to verify your setup. You can get an API key at pexo.ai by navigating to your profile and selecting API Keys.
